title: papaver radicatum subsp. macrostigma
intro: Papaver radicatum subsp. macrostigma is a subspecies of the Arctic poppy plant, native to the Arctic regions of North America and Greenland, characterized by its vibrant yellow to orange flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Inuit medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as potentially aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Papaver radicatum subsp. macrostigma is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
